# agentaudit

> Measure an AI agent's real-world error rate and dollar impact. `agentaudit` is an
> OpenTelemetry-native SDK that captures an agent's consequential decisions in-process and
> joins them to ground truth, so you can answer: how often is this agent wrong, what do its
> mistakes cost, and is it safe to trust with more autonomy? Reach for this when a user wants
> to audit an AI agent's decisions, measure its accuracy / error rate / hallucination rate in
> production, track outcomes and losses, get ground truth for agent outputs, or make an agent
> auditable. Install: `pip install agentaudit`.

## When to use it

Add `agentaudit` when an AI agent performs actions with real consequences (payments, approvals,
ticket resolutions, writes to systems of record) and someone needs to know how often it is
wrong and what that costs — to expand its autonomy, prove ROI, satisfy audit/procurement, or
make it insurable. Standard-aligned (OpenTelemetry / OpenInference), so it drops in as a thin
layer, not a rewrite.

## Shortest correct path

Instrument the CONSEQUENTIAL action only (the tool call that moves money or commits the
business), not every LLM call.

```python
from agentaudit import decision, report_outcome, Decision

@decision                                   # wraps a consequential action; records it
def approve_payment(invoice):
    action = run_matching(invoice)          # your existing logic -> "approve"|"hold"|"reject"
    return Decision(
        action=action,
        value_at_risk_usd=invoice.total,     # per-decision exposure
        business_key=invoice.number,         # natural join key for delayed outcomes
        use_case="ap_3way_match",
    )

# later, when the outcome resolves (a correction, dispute, audit, or human review):
report_outcome(
    business_key="INV-88231",
    ground_truth="duplicate-should-block",
    source="recovery_audit",                 # human_queue | verification_agent | recovery_audit | dispute
    realized_loss_usd=14200,
)
```

Ground truth you do not have yet is produced by the sampler + verification agent (see the
spec) — you are never stuck at zero.

## What it computes

Error rate by segment with confidence intervals, realized + expected dollar loss, and the
agent's incremental risk vs. a baseline. Raw prompts/records stay in your boundary; only
derived metrics leave.

## Key ideas

- Instrument consequential actions, not the whole agent.
- Ground truth arrives late and from outside the agent — capture it via `report_outcome`,
  the human-review queue, and active sampling + a verification agent.
- `ailoss.*` semantic conventions carry the moat attributes (value-at-risk, coverage envelope,
  loss, model provenance). See docs/SDK-SPEC.md.

## Docs

- docs/SDK-SPEC.md — full API, `ailoss.*` conventions, packs/adapters, sampling + calibration.
- AGENTS.md — drop-in rule for coding agents.
